steps.title: Set up the logic
steps.content: P6 gave equal ratings to abstract and cubist paintings. The model's predictions are validated by the correlation in the graph. If P6 has a low correlation for those styles, the model would not predict equal ratings.

steps.title: Match the data
steps.content:
When a participant's correlation between predicted and actual is poor for a style pair, the model's predicted ratings would differ from the actual equal ratings. So the model would predict different (not equal) ratings for P6's abstract vs. cubist paintings.

A says they'd equal one another (no — that's what the actual ratings did, not the predictions). C and D pick a direction the data don't support.

That matches choice B — B is correct.
